title: Understanding Centripetal Acceleration
text: Centripetal acceleration is the acceleration required to keep an object moving in a circular path. It is directed towards the center of the circle.

title: Formula for Centripetal Acceleration
text: The formula for centripetal acceleration is given by \(a_c=v**2/r\), where \(v\) is the velocity and \(r\) is the radius of the circular path.

title: Substitute the Values
text: Substitute the given values into the formula \(a_c=v**2/r\). Use $$\(v=89.0\)$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$ and $$\(r=200.0\)$$ $$m$$.
